adjacent_excluded_source.reason: Separate cross-axis sampler with six top-level blocks, no Axis0 lattice, and different descendant naming.

notes: This batch keeps the focused engine32 attack lattice separate from the adjacent full-axis sampler.
No current top-level evidence-pack block was found for S_SIM_ENGINE32_AXIS0_AXIS6_ATTACK.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Shows engine32 as its own cataloged result surface and full_axis_suite as a separate OTHER surface.
Contains current standalone descendant evidence for the adjacent full-axis sampler family but no direct engine32 top-level block.
Paired result for the adjacent excluded next-family source.
Current standalone Axis3 descendant of the adjacent sampler family.
Current standalone Axis4 descendant of the adjacent sampler family.
Current standalone Axis5 FGA descendant of the adjacent sampler family.
Current standalone Axis5 FSA descendant of the adjacent sampler family.
Current standalone Axis6 descendant of the adjacent sampler family.

runtime_expectations: run_engine32_axis0_axis6_attack.py writes one results_engine32_axis0_axis6_attack.json and one sim_evidence_pack.txt with 1 SIM_EVIDENCE block.
The result lattice spans 2 type signs x 4 sequences x 2 loop orientations x 2 mix modes for 32 keyed cells.
All cells share seed 0, states 256, cycles 16, theta 0.07, terrain params gamma 0.12 / p 0.08 / q 0.10, and operator params theta_te 0.05 / q_fi 0.06 / d_fe 0.04.
The evidence block emits only stage-level MIX_R minus UNIFORM deltas.
failure_modes: The family can be over-read as AB-coupled Axis0 evidence even though the implementation explicitly uses a one-qubit proxy.
The evidence block can be mistaken for the full result surface even though it compresses absolute values into deltas.
Catalog presence can be mistaken for top-level evidence-pack admission.
The adjacent full-axis sampler can be merged incorrectly into this family because of raw-order adjacency and partial axis overlap.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
